description: Structural (decrypted/pre-encryption) known-answer vectors for the ldgr sync wire format. The on-the-wire sync blob is json(encrypt_item(vault_key, json(EventBatch))); encrypt_item uses a random item key and random nonce per call, so the ciphertext is NOT byte-reproducible. These vectors therefore pin the deterministic *structural* layer every client (CLI / iOS UniFFI / web WASM) must produce byte-for-byte: the EventBatch envelope, the per-entity payloads carried inside each event, and the SRP register request body. Each file is the exact compact JSON the canonical Rust client emits. Regenerate after a reviewed format change with: LDGR_REGENERATE_VECTORS=1 cargo test -p ldgr-core --all-features --test sync_vectors

vectors.name: account_payload_v1.json
vectors.description: Full account state carried inside a Create/Update account event (SyncEvent.payload).

vectors.name: transaction_payload_v1.json
vectors.description: Full transaction state (with ordered postings) carried inside a Create/Update transaction event.

vectors.name: event_batch_v1.json
vectors.description: The complete pre-encryption batch envelope (device_id, ordered events, BTreeMap vector clock). This is the exact plaintext sealed by framing::seal_batch. Event payloads appear as JSON byte arrays (the UTF-8 bytes of the inner payload JSON), matching the web outbox's Array.from(TextEncoder().encode(...)) encoding.

vectors.name: register_request_1secret_v1.json
vectors.description: Legacy single-secret SRP registration body. auth_scheme is omitted from the wire (skip_serializing_if), keeping it byte-identical to the original protocol.

vectors.name: register_request_2skd_v1.json
vectors.description: Two-secret (2SKD, ADR-008) registration body, with auth_scheme set to srp-2skd-v1.
